summary.intent: Fix a bug where auth_url was being read from the wrong response property path, and resolve outstanding Greptile code review comments on a PR.
summary.outcome: Fixed both occurrences of `res.result.auth_url` (with and without optional chaining) in JS files and committed the fix; confirmed 4 other Greptile comments were stale/already addressed in prior commits.

repo: The project uses Greptile for automated code review with iterative review cycles triggered manually.
Blocking I/O (std::fs) is acceptable in #[cfg(test)] only; production code must use tokio::fs.
Response payload shape uses res.payload?.auth_url, not res.result.auth_url — the two properties are distinct and the guard path must match the read path.

code.finding: Had res.result.auth_url (without optional chaining) that needed to be updated to match the guarded res.payload?.auth_url pattern.
Same auth_url path mismatch as page-channels.js — window.open was reading from res.result.auth_url instead of res.payload.auth_url.

workflow: replace_all edits only match the exact string provided — a variant with optional chaining (?.) is a different string from one without, requiring separate edits.

Greptile review comments reference line numbers at time of review; after a new commit, old comments may point to stale/already-fixed locations and should be verified against current code before acting on them.
Triggering a second Greptile review iteration after pushing fixes is useful to confirm remaining issues vs. stale comments.

friction: A replace_all operation silently missed the non-optional-chaining variant (res.result.auth_url) because it only matched res.result?.auth_url, requiring a manual follow-up grep and two separate edits.
Greptile re-reviews reference stale line numbers after new commits, requiring manual verification to distinguish real issues from already-fixed ones.
